title: INtech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Jessie Diggins’ Starter Pack: Winter Olympics 2026
items.description: What a three-time Olympic medalist packs to Milano Cortina 2026.

items.title: AI Industry Rivals Are Teaming Up on a Startup Accelerator
items.description: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and a host of other major tech companies have found common ground in F/ai, a new startup accelerator based out of Paris.

items.title: Samsung to hold its Galaxy S26 event on February 25
items.description: Samsung is going to launch S26 lineup just before Mobile World Congress (MWC).

items.title: Wacom MovinkPad 11 Tablet Review: A Portable Sketch Pad
items.description: Wacom’s Android-based drawing pad tries, and mostly succeeds, to pack a full digital art workstation into a portable sketch pad.

items.title: OpenAI policy exec who opposed chatbot’s “adult mode” reportedly fired on discrimination claim
items.description: The executive has denied the allegation that she engaged in discrimination.

items.title: Okay, now exactly half of xAI’s founding team has left the company
items.description: Whatever the cause of the departures, the cumulative impact is alarming. There is a lot of work left to do at xAI, and an IPO will bring more scrutiny...

items.title: With co-founders leaving and an IPO looming, Elon Musk turns talk to the moon
items.description: According to The New York Times, which reports that it heard the meeting, Musk told employees that xAI needs a lunar manufacturing facility, a factory...

items.title: Mazzer Philos Review (2026): Sweet, Zero-Retention Grinds
items.description: Mazzer Philos is a commercial-caliber coffee grinder made for the home countertop. And it made for some of the most delicious espresso I pulled this y...

items.title: Razer Huntsman V3 Pro 8KHz Review: A Keyboard for the Competitive
items.description: This esports keyboard isn't particularly comfortable, but it's incredibly competitive.

items.title: Amazon may launch a marketplace where media sites can sell their content to AI companies
items.description: A new report claims the e-commerce giant is looking to create a pipeline of licensable content between media publishers and AI companies.
